query: ok i've been coding all day and i have like 20 files changed. can you just look at my git status and help me commit everything in a sensible way? i don't want one giant commit

query: help me commit this

query: git commit time -- i refactored the db layer and also fixed a bug in the user service. how should i structure this?

notes: Merge conflict resolution, not committing

notes: Git amend operation -- near miss because it involves modifying a commit, but is a different workflow from creating new commits

query: can you help me write a good squash commit message for this PR? I want to combine all 8 commits into one clean message before merging into main

notes: PR title/description writing, not a git commit message -- near miss because it's writing a description of code changes

query: I need to revert the last commit because it broke production. How do I do that?
notes: git revert creates a new commit with a revert: type -- the skill explicitly covers the revert commit type in its type table. This should trigger.
